name: Electroweak oblique parameters S, T, U
requirement: Any candidate framework must yield electroweak oblique parameters S, T, U consistent with the post-2024 EW global fit. After ATLAS and CMS m_W measurements brought the W mass into agreement with the SM, the global fit gives S = -0.05 +/- 0.07, T = 0.00 +/- 0.06, U = 0.00 +/- 0.05 (with U fixed = 0 in many fits to break degeneracy).

regime.description: Electroweak scale; oblique BSM contributions to W, Z, photon self-energies parameterize universal heavy-new-physics effects.

current_bound.experiment: Global EW fit (Z resonance LEP/SLC + LHC m_W + LHC m_t + Higgs mass)
current_bound.reference: Particle Data Group 2024 Electroweak Review; de Blas et al. (HEPfit), 'The Global Electroweak and Higgs Fits in the LHC era', PoS EPS-HEP2017, 467 (2017), arXiv:1710.05402; updated with post-2024 m_W

what_it_excludes: Strongly constrains universal heavy-new-physics (technicolor, generic 4th generation, heavy Z'). The CDF II m_W result alone preferred T ~ 0.15-0.20 (3 sigma); incorporating ATLAS+CMS+LHCb brings T back to zero, removing the motivation for many BSM proposals from 2022-2023.

parameterization.name: Peskin-Takeuchi oblique parameters S, T, U from gauge-boson self-energy corrections; equivalently SMEFT operators O_HD (T), O_HWB (S), O_HWB + dimension-8 (U).
parameterization.reference: Peskin and Takeuchi, PRD 46, 381 (1992); Wells and Zhang, JHEP 04, 042 (2017), arXiv:1611.04484 (SMEFT translation).
parameterization.summary: BSM heavy fields contribute through transverse vacuum polarizations of W, Z; reduced to three numbers in the leading-order universal-new-physics expansion.

procedural.predicted_observable: Pre-EWSB self-energy corrections to W, Z, photon, computed from new heavy-particle loops. Project onto S, T, U via standard formulas.
procedural.bound_in_parameterization: S, T, U all consistent with 0 within ~0.1, equivalently new-physics scale Lambda_NP > ~5-10 TeV for O(1) couplings.
framework_inputs: Compute the framework's contributions to gauge-boson self-energies (W^+ W^-, Z Z, gamma gamma, gamma Z) at one loop.
Translate into S, T, U via the standard Peskin-Takeuchi formulas.

primary_references.citation: Peskin and Takeuchi, 'Estimation of oblique electroweak corrections', PRD 46, 381 (1992)
Wells and Zhang, 'Renormalization group evolution of the universal theories EFT', JHEP 04, 042 (2017)
de Blas, Pierini, Reina, Silvestrini, 'Impact of the recent measurements of the top-quark and W-boson masses on electroweak precision fits', PRL 129, 271801 (2022)
